requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Branch Authorisation) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Branch Authorisation) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence: These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.
requirements.object: immediate effect

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks (RCBs),
hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank'.
requirements.object: Co-operative Banks (RCBs), hereinafter collectively referred to as 'banks' and individually as a 'bank'

requirements.full_sentence:
In this context, 'Rural Co-operative Banks' shall mean State Co-operative Banks
(StCBs) and Central Co-operative Banks (CCBs), as defined in the National
Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981.
requirements.object: (StCBs) and Central Co-operative Banks (CCBs), as defined in the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act

requirements.full_sentence:
In these Directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Area of Operation' of a bank means the geographical area/s of operation
stated in its byelaws, as approved by the registering authority and the
Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: to them below: (1) 'Area of Operation' of a bank means the geographical area/s of operation stated in its byelaws, as appro

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) 'Administrative Office' or 'Controlling Office' means a corporate,
regional, zonal, or any other office, by whatsoever name called, that
exercises control or oversight functions on Branches / Offices falling under
its jurisdiction and undertakes internal administrative functions including
oversight of bank's own staff and carries out no banking or business
transactions.
requirements.object: or any other office, by whatsoever name called, that exercises control or oversight functions on Branches / Offices fall

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) 'Back Offices' means a Central Processing Centre (CPC) or an Office, by
whatever name called, that exclusively attends to functions such as data
processing, processing of loans, verification and processing of documents,
issuance of cheque books, demand drafts etc.
requirements.object: (CPC) or an Office, by whatever name called, that exclusively attends to functions such as data processing, processing of

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by RBI or as used in commercial
parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or any statutory mod

conditions:
if it meets the following
conditions
requirements.full_sentence:
A bank will be considered as fully complying with ECBA if it meets the following
conditions, based on the audited financial statements as of 31st March of the
immediately preceding financial year:
(1) The CRAR should be at least one percentage point above the minimum
CRAR applicable to the bank as on the reference date;
requirements.object: percentage point above the minimum CRAR applicable to the bank as on the reference date

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(6) The bank should not be under any Directions / Supervisory Action
Framework / PCA of RBI / NABARD, as the case may be, at the time of Board
resolution declaring the bank as ECBA compliant/ application for
authorization;
requirements.object: of Board resolution declaring the bank as ECBA compliant/ application for authorization

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank shall determine its compliance with the ECBA every year based on the
audited financial statements as of 31 March of the immediately preceding FY and
place it before its Board within 30 days from the date of adoption of the audit
report.
requirements.object: the ECBA every year based on the audited financial statements as of 31 March of the immediately preceding FY and place i

requirements.timeline:
within 15 calendar days from the date of the Board resolution
as per the format given in Annex I
requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall satisfy itself about the compliance of the bank with ECBA
and pass the necessary resolution approving the same and inform the Reserve
Bank and NABARD within 15 calendar days from the date of the Board resolution
as per the format given in Annex I.
requirements.object: compliance of the bank with ECBA and pass the necessary resolution approving the same and inform the Reserve Bank and NABARD

conditions: In case during the supervisory review of NABARD
requirements.full_sentence:
In case during the supervisory review of NABARD, or even
otherwise, if a bank, which has declared itself compliant with ECBA is found to
be non-compliant, the bank shall be subject to appropriate supervisory and / or
enforcement action as deemed fit by the Reserve Bank, including but not limited
to debarment from self-reviewing itself as ECBA compliant for a period of
minimum of one year.
requirements.object: supervisory and / or enforcement action as deemed fit by the Reserve Bank, including but not limited to debarment from self-

requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, DCCBs desirous
of amending their area of operation shall approach the Reserve Bank along with
the notification from the State Government, a resolution passed to this effect by
their Board and the amended byelaws registered with Registrar of Co-operative
Societies (RCS).
requirements.object: along with the notification from the State Government, a resolution passed to this effect by their Board and the amended bye

requirements.full_sentence:
DCCBs do not require NOC from the Reserve Bank for
amendment of byelaws in this regard and may directly approach the Reserve
Bank for amendment in the banking license with the above-mentioned
documents.
requirements.object: Bank for amendment in the banking license with the above-mentioned documents

requirements.full_sentence:
The reporting of
opening a new place of business shall be as per procedure given in Chapter III of
this Master Direction.
requirements.object: given in Chapter III of this Master Direction

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should ensure that there are no restrictions imposed by the local
development or other authorities for setting up a commercial establishment in the
locality where the new place of business is proposed to be opened.
requirements.object: no restrictions imposed by the local development or other authorities for setting up a commercial establishment in the locality

requirements.full_sentence:
Prior Approval Route: For opening of branches of
more than 15% allowed under automatic route, banks
which are in compliance with ECBA, may apply for
opening branches by submitting an Annual Business
Plan (ABP), subject to additional conditions at Para 20
to 24.
requirements.object: by submitting an Annual Business Plan (ABP)

requirements.full_sentence:
b) Opening of an EC A bank in compliance with ECBA may open ECs
under both automatic route and prior approval route,
subject to additional conditions at para 25 to 28 below.
requirements.object: automatic route and prior approval route

requirements.full_sentence:
Prior Approval Route: A bank may apply for opening
ECs by submitting an Annual Business Plan (ABP).
requirements.object: by submitting an Annual Business Plan (ABP)

requirements.full_sentence:
d) Opening of a Central
A bank may open such offices within its area of
operation as per its business requirements, without
Processing Centre,
prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: its area of operation as per its business requirements, without Processing Centre, prior approval of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The application may be
(Regional / Zonal /
submitted as a part of ABP.
requirements.object: / submitted as a part of ABP

requirements.full_sentence:
Administrative Office)
#
# Such offices should not be involved in business origination.
requirements.object: origination

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank is eligible to open a branch in its area of operation (including the
upgradation of an extension counter that is in operation for more than three years)
under the automatic route subject to the following conditions:
(1) The bank shall be in compliance with ECBA;
requirements.object: ECBA

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) The bank should have sufficient headroom capital (methodology given in Part
C of Annex II).
requirements.object: (methodology given in Part C of Annex II)

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall put in place a policy for opening branches, approved by its Board
of Directors.
requirements.object: policy for opening branches, approved by its Board of Directors

requirements.full_sentence:
While formulating the said policy, the bank shall bear in mind its
financial health, viability of the proposed branches and bank's ability to render
satisfactory customer service.
requirements.object: financial health, viability of the proposed branches and bank's ability to render satisfactory customer service

requirements.full_sentence:
The policy must be reviewed periodically to keep it
aligned with the evolving banking landscape and revision, if any, in Reserve Bank
guidelines.
requirements.object: keep it aligned with the evolving banking landscape and revision

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, the bank shall ensure that the proposal for opening of such
branches in a particular financial year, based on the policy, is duly approved by its
Board of Directors.
requirements.object: for opening of such branches in a particular financial year, based on the policy, is duly approved by its Board of Directors

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks in compliance with ECBA and desirous of opening of branches of more than
15 per cent allowed under automatic route, may apply for opening branches under
the Prior Approval Route by submission of a single Annual Business Plan (ABP)
(format given in Annex II) in a financial year, along with application as per Form V
of the Banking Regulation (Co-operative Societies) Rules, 1966.
requirements.object: under the Prior Approval Route by submission of a single Annual Business Plan (ABP) (format given in Annex II) in a financi

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall
have a policy as at para 19.
requirements.object: at para 19

requirements.full_sentence: ABP for a financial year, should be submitted by the bank well in advance.
requirements.object: bank well in advance

requirements.full_sentence:
The approval granted by the
Reserve Bank for opening the branch will be valid till March 31 of concerned
financial year, by which time the branch must be operationalised, or else the
approval will lapse automatically.
requirements.object: the approval will lapse automatically

requirements.full_sentence:
Non-operationalisation of at least 75 per cent of approved
branches within permitted time would be viewed seriously and may impact the
approvals in future, including debarment from opening new branches for the next
two years.
requirements.object: future, including debarment from opening new branches for the next two years

conditions: In case a branch is required in district headquarters
requirements.full_sentence:
In case a branch is required in district headquarters, the bank may
open a branch only for maintaining and servicing deposits of co-operative banks.
requirements.object: for maintaining and servicing deposits of co-operative banks

requirements.full_sentence:
In semi-urban / rural areas, where DCCB is non-existent, weak or virtually defunct,
StCB may be allowed to open a branch, provided the DCCB in the adjoining district
is not in a position to extend its area of operation to cover the area (where DCCB
is non-existent in the concerned district) or to take over the weak / defunct DCCB
through merger.
requirements.object: a branch

requirements.full_sentence:
Such StCB shall clearly justify the reasons for opening each
branch / extension counter while applying under ABP.
requirements.object: for opening each branch / extension counter while applying under ABP

requirements.full_sentence:
In a two-tier co-operative
credit structure consisting of StCB and PACS, a StCB may open branch /
extension counters in urban as well as rural areas.
requirements.object: counters in urban as well as rural areas

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the StCB shall also
clearly justify the reasons for opening each branch / extension counter while
applying under ABP.
requirements.object: reasons for opening each branch / extension counter while applying under ABP

requirements.full_sentence:
An EC may be opened within the premises of educational institutions, big offices,
factories and hospitals of which the co-operative bank concerned is the principal
banker.
requirements.object: premises of educational institutions, big offices, factories and hospitals of which the co-operative bank concerned is the princi

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall obtain a declaration from the institution in which it proposes
to open EC in the format as per Annex IV.
requirements.object: the institution in which it proposes to open EC in the format as per Annex IV

requirements.full_sentence:
Other bankers to such entities may also
open an EC provided it is not considered feasible by the principal banker and / or
the base branch of the principal banker is beyond 10 kilometres from the proposed
EC, after obtaining written consent from the principal banker.
requirements.object: provided it is not considered feasible by the principal banker and / or the base branch of the principal banker is beyond 10 kilometre

requirements.full_sentence:
An EC may also be
opened in the residential colonies provided no other branch / EC is already existing
in the colony and no restrictions have been imposed by the local development or
any other authorities for setting up a commercial establishment in the residential
colony / locality.
requirements.object: the residential colonies provided no other branch / EC is already existing in the colony and no restrictions have been imposed by th

requirements.full_sentence:
No extension counter should be opened in a marketplace,
shopping centre, etc.
requirements.object: marketplace, shopping centre, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Base branch of the bank to which the proposed EC is linked should be within a
distance of 10 kilometres.
requirements.object: of 10 kilometres

requirements.full_sentence:
The transactions carried out in the EC should be
incorporated in the accounts of the base branch on a day-to-day basis.
requirements.object: accounts of the base branch on a day-to-day basis

requirements.full_sentence:
While opening an EC, the bank should take into account important factors such as
need, viability, and overall merits of opening the EC, including the bank's ability to
deploy adequate resources and exercise internal controls.
requirements.object: factors such as need, viability, and overall merits of opening the EC, including the bank's ability to deploy adequate res

requirements.full_sentence:
The facilities at an EC should be restricted to:
(1) deposit / withdrawal transactions;
requirements.object: deposit / withdrawal transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may offer all its products and services through the ATM channels, provided
the technology permits and adequate checks are put in place.
requirements.object: and services through the ATM channels

requirements.full_sentence:
The business
transacted at the ATM shall be recorded in the books of the respective base branch
/ Centralised Data Centre.
requirements.object: books of the respective base branch / Centralised Data Centre

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the bank may utilize the ATM screens to display their own
products, or products for which it acts as an agent, or customer service / financial
inclusion / cyber safety messages.
requirements.object: to display their own products, or products for which it acts as an agent, or customer service / financial inclusion / cyber sa

requirements.full_sentence:
These guidelines shall also be applicable to
CDMs and CRMs.
requirements.object: CDMs and CRMs

requirements.full_sentence:
The decision to shift a place of business shall be taken by the Board after taking
into account all the relevant factors, including viability, and should be properly
recorded / minuted in the proceedings of the Board meeting.
requirements.object: Board after taking into account all the relevant factors, including viability, and should be properly recorded / minuted in the procee

requirements.full_sentence:
Proper notice should be given to all existing depositors / clients of the branch / EC
through SMS / public notice / letters at least one month in advance of shifting.
requirements.object: existing depositors / clients of the branch / EC through SMS / public notice / letters at least one month in advance of shifting

requirements.full_sentence:
Closing a branch / EC in one city / town and opening in another shall not be
construed as shifting.

However, a bank may shift its office, except Head Office,
from one town / city to another within its area of operation, without prior approval
of the Reserve Bank.

Closure of a
branch / extension counter shall be subject to the following conditions:
(1) The bank should not have been placed under any Directions under section
35A of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (AACS).
requirements.object: following conditions: (1) The bank should not have been placed under any Directions under section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act,

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The decision to close down the branch / EC should be taken by the Board after
taking into account all the relevant factors and should be properly noted in the
official record of proceedings of the Board meeting.
requirements.object: Board after taking into account all the relevant factors and should be properly noted in the official record of proceedings of the Boa

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The bank should give two months' notice in advance to all existing depositors
/ clients of the branch, including through press releases in leading local
newspapers, as well as communicate to each constituent of the branch well in
advance of the closure of the branch.
requirements.object: in advance to all existing depositors / clients of the branch, including through press releases in leading local newspapers, a

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The bank should report to the Reserve Bank, as per the timeline (reporting
format given in Annex III), along with copies of the Board resolution.
requirements.object: Bank, as per the timeline (reporting format given in Annex III), along with copies of the Board resolution

requirements.timeline: within 15 calendar days of closing the branch
requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The bank should surrender the branch license in original, wherever applicable,
to the Reserve Bank within 15 calendar days of closing the branch.
requirements.object: in original

requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The bank should preserve all the relevant records and make them available to
the Reserve Bank / NABARD for scrutiny.
requirements.object: records and make them available to the Reserve Bank / NABARD for scrutiny

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank may provide doorstep banking services to its customers on a voluntary
basis, without prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: to its customers on a voluntary basis, without prior approval of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank may formulate a scheme for providing doorstep banking services to its
customers, with the approval of their Boards, in accordance with the guidelines
provided in Annex V.
requirements.object: providing doorstep banking services to its customers, with the approval of their Boards, in accordance with the guidelines prov

requirements.timeline: within 15 calendar days of implementing the scheme
requirements.full_sentence:
The details of the scheme may be informed to the Reserve
Bank within 15 calendar days of implementing the scheme.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank within 15 calendar days of implementing the scheme

requirements.full_sentence:
A bank having deposit of more than ₹1000 crore as on March 31 of previous
financial year may offer doorstep banking services to customers either directly
through its own employees or through agents.
requirements.object: to customers either directly through its own employees or through agents

requirements.full_sentence:
Other banks shall offer doorstep
banking services only through their permanent employees.
requirements.object: only through their permanent employees

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank shall take
into account the various risks that may arise on account of offering Doorstep
Banking Services, such as outsourcing risk, operational risk, reputational risk, etc.,
to customers either directly through its own employees or through agents and take
all necessary steps to manage the same.
requirements.object: various risks that may arise on account of offering Doorstep Banking Services, such as outsourcing risk, operational risk, reput

requirements.full_sentence:
The operation of the scheme may be reviewed by the Board of the bank on an
annual basis.
requirements.object: Board of the bank on an annual basis
of the Reserve Bank for: (1) Sale of bank's own premises

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks are required to obtain approval from the Reserve Bank by submitting the
application through PRAVAAH portal as per the enclosed format given in Annex
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank by submitting the application through PRAVAAH portal as per the enclosed format given in Annex

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should not enter into any firm commitment to the acquisition of
premises for shifting an office thereat until the Reserve Bank's prior approval has
been obtained.
requirements.object: commitment to the acquisition of premises for shifting an office thereat until the Reserve Bank's prior approval has been obtained

conditions: if a bank has inadvertently made any such commitment
requirements.full_sentence:
Therefore, if a bank has inadvertently made any such commitment,
it should, in its own interest, take steps to cancel or nullify it.
requirements.object: interest, take steps to cancel or nullify it

requirements.full_sentence:
Under this system, banks are required to submit their information in a
single Proforma (Annex I of CISBI) online on the CISBI portal.
requirements.object: in a single Proforma (Annex I of CISBI) online on the CISBI portal

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks should submit information on
the CISBI portal as per guidelines given in Annex VI, and thereafter, bank branch
/ office / NAIO / CSP codes would be allotted by CISBI after due validations.
requirements.object: CISBI portal as per guidelines given in Annex VI, and thereafter, bank branch / office / NAIO / CSP codes would be allotted

requirements.full_sentence:
All banks should
submit immediately and, in any case, not later than seven calendar days, the
information relating to the opening, closure, merger, shifting and conversion of
bank branches / offices / NAIOs / CSPs online through the CISBI portal.
requirements.object: any case, not later than seven calendar days, the information relating to the opening, closure, merger, shifting and conver

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, in order to ensure the correctness of data on CISBI, in the last week of
every month, banks shall generate a 'NIL Report' in CISBI for the position as on
last day of the previous month, indicating the total number of functioning branches,
offices, NAIOs, CSPs;
requirements.object: in CISBI for the position as on last day of the previous month, indicating the total number of functioning branches, offices,

requirements.full_sentence:
After
gaining first-time access to the system, banks shall ensure to submit correct and
updated bank-level information in all the fields where submission / updation rights
are available with the bank.
requirements.object: and updated bank-level information in all the fields where submission / updation rights are available with the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
After the initial submission of information on the CISBI
portal, a one-time confirmation stating that "correct and updated Bank level
information has been submitted on CISBI" shall be sent by banks to the Regional
Office concerned of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: to the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Any subsequent changes in the bank-level
information shall be submitted for updation on the CISBI portal on an immediate
basis by the banks.
requirements.object: on the CISBI portal on an immediate basis by the banks

requirements.full_sentence:
The banks shall enter the complete and clear (without
abbreviations) particulars on the Banking Channel Name, Address, geotagging,
etc.
requirements.object: clear (without abbreviations) particulars on the Banking Channel Name, Address, geotagging, etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Reporting Timeline: Reporting on the CISBI portal within the prescribed time of
seven calendar days, shall be ensured by the bank.
requirements.object: bank

requirements.full_sentence:
Any instance for which a prior
approval of the Reserve Bank is not required for any authorisation in terms of these
Directions, the bank shall report such event / changes to the Reserve Bank within
15 calendar days (reporting format given in Annex III).
requirements.object: changes to the Reserve Bank within 15 calendar days (reporting format given in Annex III)

requirements.full_sentence:
Point of contact for application / submission of information: The point of contact
for all matters related to this Direction shall be Regional Office of NABARD which,
in turn, shall forward the applications to the Regional Office of the Reserve Bank,
along with their comments and recommendations on the matter.
requirements.object: NABARD which, in turn, shall forward the applications to the Regional Office of the Reserve Bank, along with their comments and

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Banks shall submit their applications to the Reserve Bank using relevant
application forms in the PRAVAAH portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in).
requirements.object: the Reserve Bank using relevant application forms in the PRAVAAH portal (https://pravaah

requirements.full_sentence:
The Directions, instructions, and guidelines repealed prior to
the issuance of these Directions shall continue to remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.

Status (Yes / No)
1 CRAR should be at least one percentage point
above the minimum CRAR applicable
2 Net NPAs of not more than 3%
3 Net Profit during the last two financial years
No default in the maintenance of CRR / SLR during the
preceding and current financial year till the time of Board
resolution declaring the bank as ECBA compliant / application
for authorization
5 CBS fully implemented in the bank
6 Whether the bank is under Directions / Supervisory Action
Framework / PCA
7 Details, if any, of prohibition for any authorization by RBI / NABARD
Enclosures:
requirements.object: percentage point above the minimum CRAR applicable 2 Net NPAs of not more than 3% 3 Net Profit during the last two financial years No default in the maintenance of CRR / SLR during the preceding and current financial year till the time of Board resolution declaring the bank as ECBA compliant / application for authorization 5 CBS fully implemented in the bank 6 Whether the bank is under Directions / Supervisory Action Framework / PCA 7 Details

requirements.full_sentence:
Medium term policy for branch expansion programme: Bank may furnish details of
the proposed medium-term policy for its branch expansion for a period of 3 years along
with expected level of business in the next 3 years in terms of deposits and advances.
requirements.object: proposed medium-term policy for its branch expansion for a period of 3 years along with expected level of business in the next

requirements.full_sentence:
Details of proposed new branches
Name of the centre
Name of the
Members at the proposed
Population
with address and Pin
district and
centre (only for Salary Earners'
of the centre
code
state
Banks)
Note: Reasons for the proposed branch including adequacy of banking facilities at the
centre, business prospects at the proposed place of business within 12 months (an
estimate of minimum business which the bank expects to attract) may be submitted.

If yes,
details thereof may be submitted (attach extra sheet if necessary).
requirements.object: sheet if necessary)

requirements.full_sentence: Note: Separate statements may be submitted for each action.
requirements.object: action

requirements.full_sentence:
attached separately to each of the institutions, the
name and the distance of their bankers should also be indicated separately.)
∗ Strike out whichever is not applicable.

The banks which offer services of pick-up of cash shall take suitable steps to
educate their employees and agents to enable them to detect forged and mutilated
notes so as to avoid frauds and disputes with customers.
requirements.object: educate their employees and agents to enable them to detect forged and mutilated notes so as to avoid frauds and disputes with

conditions: Where banks engage the services of Agents for delivery of services
requirements.full_sentence:
Where banks engage the services of Agents for delivery of services, it should be
ensured that the policy approved by the Board lays down the broad principles for
selection of agents and payment of fee / commission etc.
requirements.object: policy approved by the Board lays down the broad principles for selection of agents and payment of fee / commission etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Banks must refer to the
guidelines on Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Managing Risks in
Outsourcing) Directions, 2025 and ensure that the principles enumerated therein are
complied with while offering Doorstep Banking Services.
requirements.object: on Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Managing Risks in Outsourcing) Directions, 2025 and ensure that the princ
a receipt on behalf of the bank

requirements.full_sentence:
b) Cash collected from the customer should be credited to the customer's account
on the same day or next working day, depending on the time of collection;
requirements.object: customer's account on the same day or next working day, depending on the time of collection

requirements.full_sentence:
c) At the time of collection of cash, the customer should be informed of the date
of credit by issuing a suitable advice;
requirements.object: date of credit by issuing a suitable advice
to the account on the basis of requisition in writing / cheque received and not against cash or instruments collected at the doorstep

requirements.full_sentence:
e) Acknowledgment should be provided for collection of KYC documents, Life
Certificate.
requirements.object: of KYC documents, Life Certificate

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall be ensured that the agreement entered into with the customer does not entail
any legal or financial liability on the bank for failure to offer doorstep services under
circumstances beyond its control.
requirements.object: agreement entered into with the customer does not entail any legal or financial liability on the bank for failure to offer doorste

requirements.full_sentence:
The services should be seen as a mere extension
of banking services offered at the branch and the liability of the bank should be the
same as if the transactions were conducted at the branch.
requirements.object: mere extension of banking services offered at the branch and the liability of the bank should be the same as if the transactions were con

requirements.full_sentence:
The agreement should not
provide any right to the customer to claim the services at his doorstep.
requirements.object: the customer to claim the services at his doorstep

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should provide cash limits (for collection as well as delivery) for their
employees / agents and customers, for doorstep banking.
requirements.object: collection as well as delivery) for their employees / agents and customers, for doorstep banking

requirements.full_sentence:
The bank should also take
all necessary steps to contain technology risk while providing these services.
requirements.object: steps to contain technology risk while providing these services

requirements.full_sentence:
Charges, if any, to be levied on the customer for doorstep services should be
incorporated in the policy approved by the Board and should form part of the
agreement entered into with the customer.
requirements.object: policy approved by the Board and should form part of the agreement entered into with the customer

requirements.full_sentence:
The charges should be prominently
indicated on the banks' website and brochures offering doorstep services.
requirements.object: the banks' website and brochures offering doorstep services

requirements.full_sentence:
Other conditions
a) Banks shall ensure compliance with the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-
operative Banks - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025 with regard to
customer identification procedures while offering doorstep services to their
customers.
requirements.object: Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co- operative Banks - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025 with regard to customer identificat

requirements.full_sentence:
b) The services should be offered at either the residence or office of the customer
as opted by the customer, the address of which should be clearly and explicitly
mentioned in the agreement.
requirements.object: the residence or office of the customer as opted by the customer, the address of which should be clearly and explicitly mentioned

requirements.full_sentence:
c) The agreement / contract with the customer shall clearly specify that the bank
will be responsible for the acts of omission and commission of its 'agent'.
requirements.object: bank will be responsible for the acts of omission and commission of its 'agent'

requirements.full_sentence:
d) Banks shall keep in view the restrictions imposed by Section 10 (1) (b) (ii) of
the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, while making payments for the services
outsourced.
requirements.object: restrictions imposed by Section 10 (1) (b) (ii) of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, while making payments for the services outsource

requirements.full_sentence:
Redressal of Grievance
a) Banks should constitute an appropriate Grievance Redressal Machinery
internally for redressing complaints about services rendered by its employees /
agents.
requirements.object: Redressal Machinery internally for redressing complaints about services rendered by its employees / agents

requirements.full_sentence:
The name and telephone number of the designated Grievance
Redressal Officer of the bank should be made available to the customers
including on the bank's website.
requirements.object: the customers including on the bank's website

requirements.full_sentence:
The designated officer should ensure that
genuine grievances of customers are redressed promptly.
requirements.object: of customers are redressed promptly

requirements.full_sentence:
Under this system, information related to Bank, Branches, Office, NAIOs, other fixed
customer service points (CSPs) (e.g., ATMs, etc.) must be submitted in CISBI.

All the banks are required to submit the above information in CISBI which will be
validated and published by the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: information in CISBI which will be validated and published by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
For getting "Bank Admin ID", a bank
should provide an authorised email ID on which the Reserve Bank can forward "Bank
Admin ID" and its password in two different emails.
requirements.object: ID on which the Reserve Bank can forward "Bank Admin ID" and its password in two different emails

requirements.full_sentence:
A new bank seeking reporting
access to CISBI, should contact the Reserve Bank with a request letter providing
details of the bank's nodal person, an email ID for receiving the login credentials and
certain basic documents as under:
a) Certificate of incorporation from the Registrar of Co-operative Societies /
Central Registrar of Co-operative Societies.
requirements.object: with a request letter providing details of the bank's nodal person, an email ID for receiving the login credentials and certa

requirements.full_sentence:
Bank should login on the CISBI portal (https://cisbi.rbi.org.in) using its allotted "Bank
Admin ID" and change the allotted password on the first login.
requirements.object: portal (https://cisbi

requirements.full_sentence:
Bank should fill all other information pertaining to the bank and submit on the CISBI
portal.
requirements.object: pertaining to the bank and submit on the CISBI portal

requirements.full_sentence:
For reporting any change in the existing information, banks should edit the existing
information and indicate the effective date of change.
requirements.object: and indicate the effective date of change

requirements.full_sentence: Banks must reset the password every three months.
requirements.object: three months

conditions:
If a bank finds any difference in the "Nil
Report" generated by CISBI and the actual status
requirements.full_sentence:
If a bank finds any difference in the "Nil
Report" generated by CISBI and the actual status, it should first update the information
in CISBI, then generate "Nil Report" and submit it through CISBI.
requirements.object: in CISBI, then generate "Nil Report" and submit it through CISBI

requirements.full_sentence:
In the last week of every month, banks shall generate 'NIL Report' for position as
on last day of previous month, authenticate it and submit on CISBI.
requirements.object: position as on last day of previous month, authenticate it and submit on CISBI

requirements.full_sentence:
For example, 'NIL
Report' for the month of June 2025 shall be generated and submitted in the last week
of July 2025.
requirements.object: in the last week of July 2025
